one_line_ask: Take the client's supplied OVERDRIVE raster logo lockup and productionize it into the full merch brand-kit deliverable set — transparent primary/stacked/icon/avatar PNGs, social-PFP crop, black/white/mono variants, a clean SVG vector source, an extracted palette reference, font recommendations, and a usage moodboard — chaining every step in Adobe.
brief: OVERDRIVE is a new scholarship-focused merchandise brand (education support via merch sales + scholarship-style campaigns); the look must read bold, modern, youthful, collegiate and merch-ready — premium streetwear, not charity/nonprofit — with the name shown as OVERDRIVE in all caps and a clean, scalable graduation-cap element (no government/university seal, lottery, or generic-education-clipart vibe). The client has approved a rendered raster logo lockup (primary horizontal version on a flat background) and supplies a hoodie mock photo for usage context. Your job, executed entirely with the Adobe connector, is to finish that concept into the production brand kit the client requested: (1) upload the supplied logo lockup into Adobe storage; (2) strip its background to a clean transparent primary logo PNG; (3) derive a tight-cropped icon/avatar from the cap mark and a square social-profile-picture crop sized for PFPs; (4) generate the merch-critical single-color variants — a full BLACK version and a full WHITE version (for dark/light garments, stickers, and watermarks) using subject selection + solid fills, plus one monochromatic brand-tint alt; (5) vectorize the transparent logo in
